children.description: The Quran's barrier between two seas, and the oceanography of density and salinity stratification.

children.title: Creation and nature
children.description: Design arguments from creation in the Quran, the Bible, and the Darwinian challenge to them.

children.title: Celestial signs: orbits, stars, iron, and light in the Quran
children.description: Orbits, star positions, plant pairs, iron, and moonlight, each honestly graded strong, flexible, or overclaimed.

islam-christianity.title: Contradictions & Difficult Questions
islam-christianity.description: A careful space for hard questions, context, fair representation, and source-backed responses.

children.title: What is a contradiction?
children.description: A framing article distinguishing genuine contradictions from textual variants, harmonizable difficulties, and interpretation.

children.title: Contradictions in the Bible
children.description: A draft map of commonly cited biblical tensions. Each item states the competing claims, why critics call it a contradiction, and the main harmonization or critical response.

children.title: Genealogies of Jesus
children.description: Why Matthew and Luke give Jesus two different genealogies, and how each tradition reads the difficulty.

children.title: The death of Judas
children.description: Matthew's hanging and Acts' falling-headlong accounts of Judas' death, and how each is read.

children.title: The timing of the crucifixion
children.description: Reconciling the Synoptic and Johannine timing of the Last Supper and crucifixion with Passover.

children.title: Did anyone see God?
children.description: Moses at Sinai, John 1:18, and Quran 6:103 and 7:143 on seeing God.

children.title: Is original sin just?
children.description: A draft comparison of inherited guilt, accountability, repentance, and justice.

children.title: Can God become man?
children.description: The Incarnation and Tawhid's objection to it, presented as a difference in starting premises about God.

children.title: Why would God allow scripture to be corrupted?
children.description: The theological objection Muslims raise about textual corruption, and whether it rebounds on the Quran.

atheism-agnosticism.title: Is belief reasonable?
atheism-agnosticism.description: From God's existence to the possibility of revelation and Islam's claim.

children.title: Does God exist?
children.description: A cumulative philosophical case from contingency, cosmic origins, order, and consciousness, with the strongest objections kept in view.

children.title: Why believe in revelation?
children.description: Why divine communication is a coherent possibility and how a claimed revelation can be tested without circular reasoning.

children.title: Why Islam and not just "a creator"?
children.description: A step-by-step bridge from generic theism to Tawhid, prophecy, Muhammad, the Quran, and a lived response.

atheism-agnosticism.title: Honest objections
atheism-agnosticism.description: Suffering and morality considered without caricaturing nonbelief.

children.title: The problem of evil
children.description: Logical, evidential, and personal dimensions of suffering, considered through freedom, natural order, test, and final justice.

children.title: Morality without God
children.description: A fair comparison of secular and theistic moral grounding that never confuses nonbelief with lack of character.

atheism-agnosticism.title: Meaning, mind, and science
atheism-agnosticism.description: Purpose, consciousness, and the relationship between science and faith.

children.title: Purpose of life
children.description: Created meaning and the Islamic claim of objective purpose through worship, stewardship, trial, and return to God.

children.title: Consciousness and the soul
children.description: What neuroscience establishes, what the hard problem asks, and what Islam affirms without pretending the soul has been scientifically detected.

children.title: Science and religion
children.description: A careful framework for naturalism, interpretation, evolution, miracles, and scientific claims without forced conflict or harmony.

atheism-agnosticism.title: The Quran's claim
atheism-agnosticism.description: A final study of the Quran's message, transmission, and guidance.

children.title: Why the Quran?
children.description: A cumulative closing case from message, Arabic form, transmission, historical emergence, and lived guidance.

people-of-palestine.title: Understanding people and place
people-of-palestine.description: Draft introductions centred on people, place, and religious connection.

children.title: Who are the people of Palestine?
children.description: A draft introduction focused on human dignity, community, and daily life rather than politics or slogans.

children.title: Why Palestine matters to Muslims
children.description: A draft on the religious, historical, and human ties Muslims feel toward Palestine, written carefully and without inflammatory language.

children.title: Jerusalem and Al-Aqsa
children.description: A draft on the significance of Jerusalem and Al-Aqsa Mosque, with historical claims clearly distinguished from religious belief.

people-of-palestine.title: Human dignity and justice
people-of-palestine.description: A draft reflection on dignity, compassion, and justice.

children.title: Human dignity and justice
children.description: A draft reflection on dignity, compassion, and justice as shared human and Islamic values.

people-of-palestine.title: Learn responsibly
people-of-palestine.description: How to assess sources and avoid propaganda while reading about Palestine.

children.title: How to learn responsibly
children.description: A draft guide for evaluating sources, avoiding propaganda, and reading history and news carefully.

children.title: Source library for Palestine
children.description: A draft reading list that separates primary sources, reference works, and current reporting.
